services.description: Top-level element defining all containers (services) to run. Each service specifies an image or build context, ports, volumes, environment variables, and other container configuration.

image.description: Specify the Docker image to start the container from. Can reference images from a registry, a local image, or include a tag or digest.

build.signature: build: <path> | build: { context: <path>, dockerfile: <file>, args: {}, target: <stage> }
build.description: Configuration options applied at build time. Can be a path string to the build context, or a detailed object specifying context, dockerfile, build args, target stage, cache settings, and more.

container_name.description: Specify a custom container name rather than a generated default. Because container names must be unique, you cannot scale a service with a custom container name.

command.description: Override the default CMD declared by the container image. Can be a shell-form string or exec-form list.

entrypoint.description: Override the default ENTRYPOINT from the image. Setting entrypoint also clears any default CMD.

ports.description: Expose container ports. Short syntax: HOST:CONTAINER or just CONTAINER. Long syntax supports protocol, mode, host_ip, and published/target fields.

expose.description: Expose ports to linked services without publishing them to the host machine. Only the internal port is specified.

environment.description: Add environment variables to the container. Can use a mapping or a list. Boolean values (true/false/yes/no) must be quoted to avoid YAML parsing issues.

env_file.description: Add environment variables from a file. Each line in the file should be in VAR=VAL format. Lines beginning with # are comments.

volumes.description: Mount host paths or named volumes. Short syntax: HOST:CONTAINER:MODE. Long syntax allows specifying type (bind, volume, tmpfs, npipe), source, target, read_only, and bind/volume options.

networks.description: Networks to join. Can be a list of network names or a mapping with per-service network configuration like aliases and static IPs.

depends_on.description: Express startup and shutdown dependencies between services. Short form is a list; long form supports conditions: service_started, service_healthy, service_completed_successfully.

restart.description: Restart policy for the container. 'no' never restarts, 'always' always restarts, 'on-failure' restarts on non-zero exit, 'unless-stopped' always restarts except when explicitly stopped.

healthcheck.signature: healthcheck: { test: <cmd>, interval: <dur>, timeout: <dur>, retries: <n>, start_period: <dur> }
healthcheck.description: Configure a check to determine if containers for this service are healthy. Test can be a string or CMD/CMD-SHELL list.

deploy.signature: deploy: { replicas: <n>, resources: { limits: {}, reservations: {} }, restart_policy: {}, ... }
deploy.description: Specify deployment and running configuration. Includes replicas, resource limits/reservations, restart policy, update/rollback config, placement constraints, and labels.

logging.description: Logging configuration for the service. Supported drivers include json-file, syslog, journald, gelf, fluentd, awslogs, splunk, none.

labels.description: Add metadata to containers using Docker labels. Can be a mapping or a list.

working_dir.description: Override the container's working directory from the image's WORKDIR.

user.description: Override the user used to run the container process. Format: user, user:group, uid, uid:gid.

secrets.signature: secrets: [<secret>, ...] | secrets: [{ source, target, uid, gid, mode }]
secrets.description: Grant access to secrets on a per-service basis. The secret must be defined in the top-level secrets section. Secrets are mounted at /run/secrets/<name> by default.

configs.signature: configs: [<config>, ...] | configs: [{ source, target, uid, gid, mode }]
configs.description: Grant access to configs on a per-service basis. The config must be defined in the top-level configs section.

network_mode.description: Set the networking mode. 'host' uses the host's networking directly. 'service:<name>' shares another service's network namespace.

cap_add.description: Add container capabilities. Example capabilities: SYS_PTRACE, NET_ADMIN, SYS_ADMIN.

cap_drop.description: Drop container capabilities. Common pattern: drop ALL then add back only needed capabilities.

privileged.description: Give extended privileges to the container. Equivalent to docker run --privileged. Use with caution for security reasons.

read_only.description: Mount the container's root filesystem as read-only. Use volumes or tmpfs for writable paths.

security_opt.description: Override the default labeling scheme for each container. Example: no-new-privileges:true, seccomp:unconfined.

pid.description: Set the PID mode. 'host' shares the host PID namespace with the container.

ipc.description: Configure IPC isolation mode. 'host' shares the host IPC namespace.

extra_hosts.description: Add hostname mappings to the container's /etc/hosts file. Use 'host-gateway' as IP to map to the host's gateway address.

tmpfs.description: Mount a temporary filesystem inside the container. Size can be limited with size option in long syntax.

ulimits.description: Override the default ulimits for a container. Common limits: nofile, nproc, memlock.

sysctls.description: Kernel parameters to set in the container. Only namespaced sysctls are supported.

stop_signal.description: Set an alternative signal to stop the container. Default is SIGTERM.

stop_grace_period.description: Time to wait for the container to stop gracefully before sending SIGKILL. Default is 10s.

init.description: Run an init process (tini) inside the container that forwards signals and reaps zombie processes.

platform.description: Define the target platform for the container. Example: linux/amd64, linux/arm64.

profiles.description: Define a list of named profiles for the service. Services with profiles are only started when a matching profile is activated.

pull_policy.description: Define when Compose should pull the image. 'always' pulls on every up, 'missing' only if not present locally, 'never' never pulls, 'build' always builds.

extends.description: Extend another service, in the current file or another, optionally overriding configuration.

develop.signature: develop: { watch: [{ action: sync|rebuild|sync+restart, path: <path>, target: <path> }] }
develop.description: Configure file watching for live development with docker compose watch. Actions: sync (copy files), rebuild (rebuild and replace), sync+restart (copy and restart).

include.description: Include other Compose files. Allows splitting a large application into sub-Compose files managed separately.

version.description: Informational Compose file format version. Deprecated — the latest Compose spec is used automatically. Common values: '3.8', '3.9', '2.4'.

name.description: Set the project name. Defaults to the directory name. Used as a prefix for container names, networks, and volumes.

driver.description: Driver for a top-level network or volume. Network drivers: bridge, overlay, host, none, macvlan, ipvlan. Volume drivers: local, or third-party.

ipam.description: Custom IPAM (IP Address Management) configuration for a network. Specify subnets, gateways, and IP ranges.

external.description: Indicate that a network or volume is managed outside of Compose. Compose does not create it and raises an error if it doesn't exist.

shm_size.description: Size of the shared memory (/dev/shm). Specified as a byte value (e.g., '2gb', '256m').

hostname.description: Declare a custom hostname for the service container.

domainname.description: Declare a custom domain name for the service container.

devices.description: List of device mappings from host to container. Permissions: r (read), w (write), m (mknod).

runtime.description: Specify the runtime to use for the container. Example: nvidia for GPU workloads.

scale.description: Set the default number of containers to deploy for this service. Deprecated in favor of deploy.replicas.

annotations.description: Add OCI annotations to the container.
